text.title: RACI Matrix Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the raci-matrix methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every task must have exactly one Accountable — no shared A, no A/A splits.
rule.rationale: Two Accountables means neither can make a final decision unilaterally; teams compromise on 'A1/A2' which hides escalation.

rule.statement: Every task must have at least one Responsible.
rule.rationale: A task with only A, C, or I has no one committed to doing the actual work.

rule.statement: Cap Consulted at 3-4 per task; more than 4 is a bottleneck signal.
rule.rationale: Each Consulted requires a round-trip communication; beyond 4 coordination overhead exceeds value.

rule.statement: Walk the RACI in the kickoff meeting cell-by-cell with all roles present.
rule.rationale: Silent paper sign-off produces false consensus; verbal walkthrough surfaces 'I didn't know I was Accountable' before work begins.

rule.statement: Use role titles, not individual names — roles survive personnel changes.
rule.rationale: Person-named matrices become stale on every transition.

rule.statement: When updating an existing RACI, output a unified diff showing what changed, not a full rewrite.
rule.rationale: Full rewrites hide which roles changed and break the audit trail.

list.item: One-person solo task with no external stakeholders
Pure agile teams with collective code ownership and one PO — flattens to PO=A everywhere
Highly emergent work where roles shift weekly — matrix decays faster than it is updated
